name: fastapi-pro
description: Build high-performance async APIs with FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2.0, and
capabilities: Design and implement async FastAPI endpoints with Pydantic V2 validation
Configure SQLAlchemy 2.0 async ORM with connection pooling and migrations
Implement JWT authentication with OAuth2 and role-based access control
Set up WebSocket connections for real-time communication features
Create comprehensive pytest test suites with async support
Deploy FastAPI applications with Docker and Kubernetes configurations
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This is a prompt-only skill containing only SKILL.md configuration file. Static analysis scanned 0 files with 0 lines of executable code and detected no security patterns. The skill provides FastAPI development guidance through natural language prompts without any code execution capabilities. No malicious patterns, external network calls, or dangerous code execution vectors were found.
valueStatement: Building high-performance async APIs requires mastering complex patterns in FastAPI, SQLAlchemy, and Pydantic. This skill provides expert guidance on modern Python async development, from API design to deployment.
limitations: Cannot execute code or run servers directly in your environment
Requires you to implement the provided code patterns in your project
Does not replace the need for understanding Python and async fundamentals
Cannot access external services or databases on your behalf

useCases.title: Backend API Development
useCases.description: Create production-ready REST APIs with proper validation, authentication, and error handling using FastAPI best practices.

useCases.title: Async Performance Optimization
useCases.description: Optimize existing synchronous APIs with async patterns, connection pooling, and caching strategies for high-concurrency workloads.

useCases.title: Real-Time Feature Implementation
useCases.description: Build WebSocket-based features like chat systems, live notifications, and streaming endpoints with proper async handling.

promptTemplates.title: Basic FastAPI Endpoint
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a FastAPI endpoint with GET and POST methods for a user resource. Include Pydantic models for request validation and response serialization with proper type hints.
promptTemplates.scenario: Starting a new FastAPI project with basic CRUD operations

promptTemplates.title: Async Database Integration
promptTemplates.prompt: Set up SQLAlchemy 2.0 async session management with asyncpg. Include connection pooling configuration, repository pattern implementation, and Alembic migration setup.
promptTemplates.scenario: Connecting FastAPI to PostgreSQL with async drivers

promptTemplates.title: JWT Authentication System
promptTemplates.prompt: Implement complete JWT authentication with access and refresh tokens. Include OAuth2 password flow, token refresh endpoint, dependency injection for protected routes, and role-based permissions.
promptTemplates.scenario: Adding secure authentication to a production API

promptTemplates.title: Microservices Architecture
promptTemplates.prompt: Design a FastAPI microservices architecture with API gateway pattern. Include inter-service communication with httpx, distributed tracing with OpenTelemetry, and Redis-based caching strategy.
promptTemplates.scenario: Scaling a monolithic API into microservices

bestPractices: Use dependency injection for database sessions, authentication, and shared services to enable testing and loose coupling
Define Pydantic models for all request/response schemas to ensure type safety and automatic OpenAPI documentation
Implement structured logging with correlation IDs for tracing requests across async operations
antiPatterns: Avoid mixing synchronous database calls in async endpoints which blocks the event loop and degrades performance
Do not store sensitive data like database URLs or API keys directly in code. Use environment variables with Pydantic Settings
Never skip input validation or rely solely on client-side checks. Always validate with Pydantic on the server

faq.question: What Python version is required for FastAPI async patterns?
faq.answer: Python 3.8+ is required. Python 3.10+ is recommended for better type hint support with union syntax and pattern matching.

faq.question: How does this skill help with database migrations?
faq.answer: Provides Alembic configuration for async SQLAlchemy, including migration generation, revision management, and integration with FastAPI startup events.

faq.question: Can this skill help with WebSocket scalability?
faq.answer: Yes. Covers WebSocket connection management, broadcasting to multiple clients, and Redis Pub/Sub for horizontal scaling across server instances.

faq.question: Does this support GraphQL alongside REST?
faq.answer: Yes. Includes guidance on integrating Strawberry or Graphene GraphQL with FastAPI, sharing Pydantic models, and managing both API types in one application.

faq.question: What testing frameworks does this skill recommend?
faq.answer: pytest with pytest-asyncio for async tests, TestClient for integration testing, factory_boy for test data generation, and pytest-cov for coverage reporting.

faq.question: How does this skill handle production deployment?
faq.answer: Provides Docker multi-stage builds, Uvicorn/Gunicorn worker configuration, Kubernetes Helm charts, and environment-based configuration with Pydantic Settings.
